intervals.name: Warmup
intervals.purpose: Prepare system and elevate heart rate

coachingNotes.TOUGH_LOVE: Rep 1/1. 15:00 at 75%. This is the price of being better. Pay it.
coachingNotes.TECHNICAL: 0% complete. Rep 1/1. 75% at 85-95 rpm. Hold form — don't let it drift.
coachingNotes.MOTIVATIONAL: 0 minutes of work done. 45 minutes to go. You can do this.
coachingNotes.DARK_HUMOR: Rep 1/1. 75% FTP. Your legs filed a complaint. It's under review. (It's not.)
coachingNotes.MIXED: Rep 1/1. 75% FTP, 85-95 rpm. Controlled effort. Execute the plan.

coachingNotes.TOUGH_LOVE: First rep. 100% FTP. Your body is uncomfortable. Your mind is stronger. Prove it.
coachingNotes.TECHNICAL: Interval 1/1. 93-100% FTP, 85-95 rpm, Seated. Execute with precision.
coachingNotes.MOTIVATIONAL: Interval 1/1. You're starting fresh. Show yourself what you're capable of.
coachingNotes.DARK_HUMOR: First rep. Your legs feel fresh. Check back in 35:00.
coachingNotes.MIXED: Interval 1/1. Power: 93-100%, Cadence: 85-95 rpm, Position: Seated. Execute.

intervals.name: Cool-down
intervals.purpose: Gradually lower heart rate and begin recovery

coachingNotes.TOUGH_LOVE: Interval 1/1. 50 minutes in. Your legs are lying. You have more.
coachingNotes.TECHNICAL: Interval 1 of 1. Target: 60% FTP, 85-95 rpm. 0 intervals remaining.
coachingNotes.MOTIVATIONAL: Interval 1/1. Every rep makes you stronger. Keep pushing. 0 more to go.
coachingNotes.DARK_HUMOR: Interval 1/1. 50 minutes in. The good news: almost done. The bad news: 0 reps left.
coachingNotes.MIXED: Interval 1. 0 to go. 0 minutes total. You've got this.
